title: NextEra Energy sells $3.75B junior subordinated debentures with step-up rates
summary: NextEra Energy (NEE) has sold $3.75 billion of junior subordinated debentures across three series (AA, BB, CC) with initial fixed coupons ranging from 6.000% to 6.625%. After an initial period, rates will reset every five years based on the Five-Year Treasury plus a margin, but will not fall below the initial rates. These debentures are subordinated, guaranteed by NextEra Energy, and have optional redemption periods beginning in 2031, 2036, and 2046 respectively.

title: How 3 Top Dividend Managers Are Navigating Today’s Market
summary: Three top dividend fund managers, Michael Barclay, Andrew Brandon, and Ramona Persaud, discussed the evolving landscape of dividend investing at the Morningstar Investment Conference. title: Jefferies (JEF) offers autocallable contingent-coupon notes linked to Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000, SMH
summary: Jefferies Financial Group Inc. has launched Senior Autocallable Contingent Coupon Barrier Notes due December 29, 2032, linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, Russell 2000 Index, and VanEck Semiconductor ETF, with payments tied to the worst-performing underlying.
